'''Yunkor IX''' was an astronomical object<ref name="X-Wing CDROM">''[[X-Wing Collector's CD-ROM: The Official Strategy Guide]]''</ref> in the [[Yunkor system/Legends|Yunkor system]],<ref name="Atlas" /> a part of the [[Jjannex sector/Legends|Jjannex sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Atlas" /> During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], a [[TIE staging area]] was located near the astronomical object. It was used to resupply battle-depleted [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial forces]] and nearby [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]].<ref name="X-Wing CDROM" />

In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="TOD Bwing Date">The [[attack on a TIE staging area]] is featured in ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Tour of Duty: B-Wing]]'', which is set after ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Tour of Duty: Imperial Pursuit]]''. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' establishes that Operation Ram's Head (in ''Imperial Pursuit'') took place approximately one year after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], which the book places in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]. Thus, the attack on a TIE staging area must take place as early as [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]. The attack on a TIE staging area is also mentioned in ''[[Star Wars Galaxies: Jump to Lightspeed]]''. ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'' is also set in 1 ABY (per the reasoning [[Template:SWG_timeline|here]]) so the attack on a TIE staging area must take place as late as 1 ABY.</ref> [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]], a [[Rebel pilot/Legends|rebel pilot]], was dispatched to the staging area in a [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing starfighter]] in order to [[Attack on a TIE staging area|destroy]] the [[Class-A cargo container|cargo containers]] and parked [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE starfighters]] there. Farlander destroyed all of his targets before jumping into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]].<ref name="X-Wing CDROM" />

==Behind the scenes==
Yunkor IX was first mentioned in the [[Star Wars: X-Wing Tour of Duty: B-Wing|''Tour of Duty: B-Wing'']] expansion to [[Totally Games]]' [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']].<ref name="TOD BWing">''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Tour of Duty: B-Wing]]''</ref> ''X-Wing'' was released in [[February]] [[1993]],<ref name="LEC history">{{LucasArts|url=20th/history_2.htm|text=20th Anniversary History, Part Two: The Classics, 1990 - 1994}}</ref> while the expansion released in the fall of that year.<ref name="Date">{{LucasFanClubCite|20|Lucasfilm's Latest}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Yunkor [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Yunkor IX, in grid square K-19.<ref name="Atlas" /> The description of the B-wing starfighter in the video game ''[[Star Wars Galaxies: Jump to Lightspeed]]''<ref name="SWG:BWCB">{{SWGcite|exp=JtL|type=item|B-Wing Chassis Blueprints}}</ref> as well as "B-Wing," a<ref name="B-Wing">{{SonyCite|url=expansions_ships_bwing.jsp|text=B-Wing}}</ref> [[September 10]], [[2004]]<ref name="Friday Features">{{SonyCite|url=content.jsp?page=Friday%20Feature%20Index|text=Friday Feature Index}}</ref> article published on the ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'' website, erroneously state that the TIE staging area was located on Yunkor IX.<ref name="SWG:BWCB" /><ref name="B-Wing" />
